title: Input Actions, Bindings, Focus, and Keyboard Equivalence · Guided Program Studio
summary: A museum puzzle team incrementally builds remappable action and focus-navigation map from original seeded fixtures while retaining earlier Python, graph, determinism, accessibility, testing, performance, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of remappable action and focus-navigation map while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.
Choose widget, class, toplevel, tag, and bindtag scope while preserving expected default behavior.
Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Action Abstraction
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop puzzle team handles case 1180 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile named actions, keyboard equivalence, focus order, and binding scope; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-input-trace-wg1807
# Competency: maze-input-trace.
# Case 9547: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_input_trace_wg1807(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_wg1807 = "wg1807-7081gw-9547"
    assert evidence_variant_wg1807
    evidence_axes_wg1807 = (
        "gridwg1807",
        "topology9547",
        "search7081gw",
        "eventwg18079547",
        "access9547wg1807",
        "recovery7081gw9547",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_wg1807)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"
